title: DevHarness Architecture Draft Output
description: Producer output contract for the DevHarness architecture draft gate before critic attack.

outcome.description: Producer must emit the single enum value that reflects the completed stage state; use blocked only when blocker explains the recoverable pause.
outcome.x-usage: Treat this as the authoritative workflow state for this output. Do not infer success from other populated fields when this says blocked or needs revision.

decision.description: Producer must state the selected architecture approach or routing conclusion, including required or deferred architecture artifacts.
decision.x-usage: Treat this as the proposed structural direction. Do not freeze it until the contract receives an approval/pass outcome.

entities.description: Producer must list architecture entities, ownership zones, or contract surfaces affected by the scoped task.
entities.x-usage: Use these as the structural surfaces that need coverage. Do not infer file-level ownership from vague entity names alone.

boundaries.description: Producer must list dependencies, seams, ownership rules, and constraints the implementation must not violate.
boundaries.x-usage: Treat these as constraints for later work unless a newer approved contract changes them. Stop and report if they conflict with the assigned task.

non_goals.description: Producer must list explicit exclusions for this architecture slice.
non_goals.x-usage: Treat these as excluded scope. Do not implement or request work for excluded items unless a newer approved contract changes them.

architecture_contract.description: Producer must draft the architecture contract for the scoped implementation, covering decisions, entities, boundaries, and non-goals.
architecture_contract.x-usage: Treat this as a draft structural contract until an approval/pass outcome freezes it. Use it to constrain later work; do not treat embedded implementation detail as edit instructions.

artifacts.description: Producer may emit named structured artifacts created by this stage when they are part of the handoff. When a research REASONS artifact exists, emit the new reasons-canvas-architecture artifact.
artifacts.x-usage: Use these as architecture-owned immutable artifacts to preserve or pass along. Do not treat them as routing decisions unless another field explicitly references them.

results.description: Producer may emit compact structured results, decisions, or evidence summaries created by this stage.
results.x-usage: Use these as supporting evidence or compact summaries. Do not let them override outcome, selected steps, blockers, or approved contracts.

summary.description: Producer must summarize the relevant decision or handoff in concise human-readable form without adding new scope.
summary.x-usage: Use this as concise human-readable context only. Do not route, approve, or expand scope from this text.

source_step_id.description: Producer must name the workflow step id that found or owns this blocker or finding.
source_step_id.x-usage: Use this to identify the step that found or owns the blocker/finding. Do not treat it as a retry target by itself.

needed.description: Producer must state the exact missing decision, permission, input, or evidence required to proceed.
needed.x-usage: Surface this exact missing input, permission, evidence, or decision before proceeding. Do not substitute assumptions for it.

evidence.description: Producer should list concrete evidence references supporting this handoff, verdict, finding, or blocker.
evidence.x-usage: Use these references to inspect or challenge the claim. Do not assume unreferenced surfaces were checked.

risk.description: Producer should describe the residual risk if this blocker or finding is ignored.
risk.x-usage: Use this to understand why ignoring the item is unsafe. Treat it as risk context unless paired with a blocked outcome.

blocker.description: Producer must populate this when outcome is blocked, naming the recoverable pause reason, owning step, and specific missing input or permission.
blocker.x-usage: Treat this as a recoverable pause. Surface the missing input or decision, then continue this same workflow step after it is resolved.
